question: My Windows login password has expired, how do I reset it?
answer: 1. Click on the Start button and select the Settings app. 2. In the Settings app, click on the 'Accounts' option. 3. Click on the 'Sign-in options' tab and then click on the 'Password' option. 4. Click on the 'Change' button and enter your current password to proceed. 5. Enter your new password and confirm it by re-entering it in the 'Confirm password' field. 6. Click on the 'Save' button to save the changes. 7. If the issue persists, contact the IT helpdesk.

question: How do I unlock a user account that has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts?
answer: 1. Check the user's login history to confirm the number of failed login attempts. 2. Verify that the user has been informed of the account lockout policy and the need to reset their password. 3. Reset the user's password using the password reset tool. 4. Once the password has been reset, have the user attempt to log in with their new password. 5. If the login is successful, the account will be unlocked automatically.

question: How do I set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on my new laptop?
answer: 1. Open the Microsoft Authenticator app on your laptop and sign in with your work account credentials. 2. Tap on the '+' icon to add a new account and select 'Work or school' as the account type. 3. Enter your work email address and tap 'Next' to proceed. 4. You will receive a prompt to scan a QR code or enter a setup key; choose the method that suits you best. 5. Once you've completed the setup process, you'll be prompted to enter a verification code sent to your registered phone or email. 6. If you've set up MFA on your phone, you can also use the Microsoft Authenticator app to receive the verification code. 7. After entering the verification code, you'll be taken to the Microsoft Authenticator app's main screen, where you can see your work account listed. 8. If the issue persists, contact the IT helpdesk.

question: Why is my Wi-Fi not connecting in the office and how do I fix it?

question: Why does Microsoft Office Word/Excel/Outlook keep crashing or not opening on startup?

question: My external monitor is not detected by the computer, what should I do?
answer: 1. Ensure that the external monitor is properly connected to the computer using a VGA, HDMI, or DisplayPort cable. Make sure the cable is securely plugged into both the monitor and the computer. 2. Restart the computer and the monitor to see if it detects the external display. This can sometimes resolve connectivity issues. 3. Check the monitor's input settings to ensure it is set to the correct input (e.g., HDMI 1, VGA, etc.). Refer to the monitor's user manual for instructions. 4. If using a laptop, try disconnecting and reconnecting the external monitor from the laptop's docking station or USB-C port. 5. If the issue persists, try using a different cable or port to rule out any hardware issues.

question: My external monitor is detected, but the image is distorted or not displaying correctly, what can I do?

If using a laptop, try updating the graphics drivers to the latest version. 5. If the issue persists, try using a different display cable or port to rule out any hardware issues.

question: I just connected an external monitor to my computer, but it's not displaying anything, what should I do?

Try changing the display mode to a different setting, such as 'Extend' or 'Duplicate', to see if it resolves the issue. 4. Check the external monitor's input settings to ensure it is set to the correct input (e.g., USB-C, HDMI, etc.). Refer to the monitor's user manual for instructions. 3. Try using a different USB-C cable or adapter to rule out any hardware issues. 4. If using a docking station, try disconnecting and reconnecting the external monitor from the docking station. 5. If the issue persists, try using a different USB-C port or a different type of connection (e.g., HDMI, DisplayPort, etc.).

question: My laptop is taking forever to start up and everything is running slowly, what could be the cause?
answer: 1. Check for any unnecessary startup programs by pressing the Windows key + R, typing 'msconfig', and clicking on the Startup tab. Disable any programs you don't need to run at startup. 2. Run the built-in Disk Cleanup tool to free up space on your hard drive. Press the Windows key + R, type 'cleanmgr', and follow the prompts. 3. Check for malware by running a full scan with your antivirus software. Make sure your antivirus software is up to date and running a full scan. 4. Check your laptop's RAM and hard drive for any issues. Run the built-in Check Disk tool by pressing the Windows key + R, typing 'chkdsk', and following the prompts. 5. If none of the above steps resolve the issue, try resetting your laptop to its default settings by pressing the Windows key + I, clicking on Update & Security, and clicking on Recovery.

question: I've noticed that my laptop is slow when I have multiple programs open at the same time, what can I do to improve performance?
answer: 1. Close any unnecessary programs to free up resources. Press the Ctrl + Shift + Esc keys to open the Task Manager and click on the Processes tab to see which programs are using the most resources. 2. Update your graphics drivers to ensure you have the latest performance-enhancing features. Press the Windows key + X and click on Device Manager, then expand the Display Adapters section and right-click on your graphics driver to update it. 3. Consider upgrading your RAM to improve multitasking performance. Check your laptop's specifications to see if it can be upgraded and purchase additional RAM if necessary. 4. Disable any visual effects that are not necessary. Press the Windows key + R, type 'sysdm.cpl', and click on the Advanced tab to disable any unnecessary visual effects. 5. If none of the above steps resolve the issue, try resetting your laptop to its default settings by pressing the Windows key + I, clicking on Update & Security, and clicking on Recovery.

question: My laptop is running slowly and I've noticed that it's overheating, what could be the cause?

If none of the above steps resolve the issue, try resetting your laptop to its default settings by pressing the Windows key + I, clicking on Update & Security, and clicking on Recovery.

question: I've installed a lot of programs on my laptop and it's running slowly, how can I free up space and improve performance?

Check for any duplicate files and delete them to free up space. Press the Windows key + E to open File Explorer and use the search function to find duplicate files. 3. Consider upgrading to a solid-state drive (SSD) for faster performance and lower power consumption. 4. Run the built-in Disk Cleanup tool to free up space on your hard drive. If none of the above steps resolve the issue, try resetting your laptop to its default settings by pressing the Windows key + I, clicking on Update & Security, and clicking on Recovery.

question: Why are my legitimate emails being sent to the spam or junk folder?

question: I’ve got the dreaded blue screen of death!
answer: Simply rebooting can fix the blue screen of death (or STOP error, as it is otherwise known).

question: I deleted some important files!
answer: The first step is to check the recycle bin. If that fails, you can contact your IT Support partner.

question: I just closed my document without saving!
answer: However, all your work is not definitely lost. If you have Auto-Recover options enabled in Microsoft Office, then there are some easy steps to recover your work. If not, you can also search for Word backup files by clicking “open”, “computer” and then browsing to the folder where the file was last saved. You may also be able to find your file by performing a search on your computer for temporary files with a .tmp file extension or a ~ prefix.

question: Slow Computer

question: My computer just shut down unexpectedly!
answer: This could be related to overheating. Check your computer for dust, and make sure it is in a cool and ventilated place. If this is not the issue, then it is likely a virus problem. Disconnect the PC from any networks and call your IT Support experts!

question: I can’t print anything!
answer: If its not an obvious issue, there may be a connection problem-the printer is probably not connected to the network properly, so call your IT Support helpdesk for help.

question: My Mouse is not working
answer: a) If you encounter a mouse problem, you should first try these options: 1) If it is a first-time issue, restarting your PC can resolve the issue instantly. 2) Confirm that the mouse or the wireless adaptor is firmly connected to the PC. 3) You may also try to unplug the mouse cable or the wireless adaptor and reconnect using a different port. 4)Check the mouse and the ports for damages and even try the mouse on a different computer. 4 If none of these solves the problem, you can now proceed to other solutions. b) Troubleshoot Hardware and Devices c) Updating Incompatible Mouse Drivers d) Roll Back or Reinstall Mouse Drivers e) Deactivate Enhanced Pointer Precision f) Adjusting the Mouse Sensitivity g) Configure Touchpad Delay h) Disable Touchpad

question: My computer does not recognise my USB device!
answer: First things to check are: 1) Does the device work in a different USB port on the machine? 2) Are other devices recognised in that port? 3) Does the device work on another user’s machine? If you have tried these troubleshooting methods and still no luck, then your IT support help-desk can proceed with some more in-depth troubleshooting.

question: My internet is really slow!
answer: If you're connecting wirelessly, then the location may be the problem. The signal is not necessarily strong in all corners of the building. Similarly, you could just be too far away. If this is not the issue, then spyware or viruses are a likely cause.
